hypothesis: When Bartley arrived at Bedford Square on Sunday evening, Marie, the pretty little French girl, met him at the door and conducted him upstairs.

reference: I SHOULD NEVER HAVE ASKED YOU IF MOLLY HAD BEEN HERE FOR I REMEMBER YOU DON'T LIKE ENGLISH COOKERY
hypothesis: I should never have asked you if Molly had been here, for I remember you don't like English cookery.

reference: I HAVEN'T HAD A CHANCE YET TO TELL YOU WHAT A JOLLY LITTLE PLACE I THINK THIS IS
hypothesis: I haven't had a chance yet to tell you what a jolly little place I think this is.

reference: THEY ARE ALL SKETCHES MADE ABOUT THE VILLA D'ESTE YOU SEE
hypothesis: They are all sketches made about the Villa d'este, you see.

reference: THOSE FELLOWS ARE ALL VERY LOYAL EVEN MAINHALL
hypothesis: Those fellows are all very loyal, even Mainhall.

reference: I'VE MANAGED TO SAVE SOMETHING EVERY YEAR AND THAT WITH HELPING MY THREE SISTERS NOW AND THEN AND TIDING POOR COUSIN MIKE OVER BAD SEASONS
hypothesis: I've managed to save something every year and that with helping my three sisters now and then and tidying poor Cousin Mike over bad seasons.

reference: IT'S NOT PARTICULARLY RARE SHE SAID BUT SOME OF IT WAS MY MOTHER'S
hypothesis: It's not particularly rare, she said, but some of it was my mother's.

reference: THERE WAS WATERCRESS SOUP AND SOLE AND A DELIGHTFUL OMELETTE STUFFED WITH MUSHROOMS AND TRUFFLES AND TWO SMALL RARE DUCKLINGS AND ARTICHOKES AND A DRY YELLOW RHONE WINE OF WHICH BARTLEY HAD ALWAYS BEEN VERY FOND
hypothesis: There was watercress soup and sole, and a delightful omelette stuffed with mushrooms and truffles, and two small rare ducklings and artichokes, and a dry yellow Rhone wine of which Bartley had always been very fond.

reference: THERE IS NOTHING ELSE THAT LOOKS SO JOLLY
hypothesis: There's nothing else that looks so jolly.

reference: THANK YOU BUT I DON'T LIKE IT SO WELL AS THIS
hypothesis: Thank you, but I don't like it so well as this.

reference: HAVE YOU BEEN IN PARIS MUCH THESE LATE YEARS
hypothesis: Have you been in Paris much these late years?

reference: THERE ARE FEW CHANGES IN THE OLD QUARTER
hypothesis: There are a few changes in the old quarter.

reference: DON'T I THOUGH I'M SO SORRY TO HEAR IT HOW DID HER SON TURN OUT
hypothesis: Don't I though? I'm so sorry to hear it. How did her son turn out?

reference: HER HAIR IS STILL LIKE FLAX AND HER BLUE EYES ARE JUST LIKE A BABY'S AND SHE HAS THE SAME THREE FRECKLES ON HER LITTLE NOSE AND TALKS ABOUT GOING BACK TO HER BAINS DE MER
hypothesis: Her hair is still like flax and her blue eyes are just like a baby's and she has the same three freckles on her little nose and talks about going back to Urban de Mer.

reference: HOW JOLLY IT WAS BEING YOUNG HILDA
hypothesis: How jolly it was being young, Hilda.

reference: DO YOU REMEMBER THAT FIRST WALK WE TOOK TOGETHER IN PARIS
hypothesis: Do you remember that 1st walk we took together in Paris?

reference: COME WE'LL HAVE OUR COFFEE IN THE OTHER ROOM AND YOU CAN SMOKE
hypothesis: Come, we'll have our coffee in the other room and you can smoke.

reference: I THINK WE DID SHE ANSWERED DEMURELY
hypothesis: I think we did, she answered demurely.

reference: WHAT SHE WANTED FROM US WAS NEITHER OUR FLOWERS NOR OUR FRANCS BUT JUST OUR YOUTH
hypothesis: What she wanted from us was neither our flowers nor our francs, but just our youth.

reference: THEY WERE BOTH REMEMBERING WHAT THE WOMAN HAD SAID WHEN SHE TOOK THE MONEY GOD GIVE YOU A HAPPY LOVE
hypothesis: They were both remembering what the woman had said when she took the money, God give you a happy love.

reference: THE STRANGE WOMAN AND HER PASSIONATE SENTENCE THAT RANG OUT SO SHARPLY HAD FRIGHTENED THEM BOTH
hypothesis: The strange woman and her passionate sentence that rang out so sharply had frightened them both.

reference: BARTLEY STARTED WHEN HILDA RANG THE LITTLE BELL BESIDE HER DEAR ME WHY DID YOU DO THAT
hypothesis: Bartley started when Hilda rang the little bell beside her. Dear me, why did you do that?

reference: IT WAS VERY JOLLY HE MURMURED LAZILY AS MARIE CAME IN TO TAKE AWAY THE COFFEE
hypothesis: It was very jolly, he murmured lazily as Marie came in to take away the coffee.

reference: HAVE I TOLD YOU ABOUT MY NEW PLAY
hypothesis: Have I told you about my new play?

reference: WHEN SHE FINISHED ALEXANDER SHOOK HIMSELF OUT OF A REVERIE
hypothesis: When she finished, Alexander shook himself out of a reverie.

reference: NONSENSE OF COURSE I CAN'T REALLY SING EXCEPT THE WAY MY MOTHER AND GRANDMOTHER DID BEFORE ME
hypothesis: Nonsense. Of course I can't really singing except the way my mother and grandmother did before me.

reference: IT'S REALLY TOO WARM IN THIS ROOM TO SING DON'T YOU FEEL IT
hypothesis: It's really too warm in this room to sing. Don't you feel it?

reference: ALEXANDER WENT OVER AND OPENED THE WINDOW FOR HER
hypothesis: Alexander went over and opened the window for her.

reference: THERE JUST IN FRONT
hypothesis: There just in front

reference: HE STOOD A LITTLE BEHIND HER AND TRIED TO STEADY HIMSELF AS HE SAID IT'S SOFT AND MISTY SEE HOW WHITE THE STARS ARE
hypothesis: He stood a little behind her and tried to steady himself as he said, It's soft and misty. See how white the stars are.

reference: FOR A LONG TIME NEITHER HILDA NOR BARTLEY SPOKE
hypothesis: For a long time neither Hilda nor Bartley spoke.

reference: HE FELT A TREMOR RUN THROUGH THE SLENDER YELLOW FIGURE IN FRONT OF HIM
hypothesis: He felt a tremor run through the slender yellow figure in front of him.

reference: BARTLEY LEANED OVER HER SHOULDER WITHOUT TOUCHING HER AND WHISPERED IN HER EAR YOU ARE GIVING ME A CHANCE YES
hypothesis: Bartley leaned over her shoulder without touching her and whispered in her ear, You are giving me a chance. Yes.

reference: ALEXANDER UNCLENCHED THE TWO HANDS AT HIS SIDES
hypothesis: Alexander clenched the two hands at his sides.

reference: THE STOP AT QUEENSTOWN THE TEDIOUS PASSAGE UP THE MERSEY WERE THINGS THAT HE NOTED DIMLY THROUGH HIS GROWING IMPATIENCE
hypothesis: The stop at Queenstown, the tedious passage up the Mersey, were things that he noted dimly through his growing impatience.

reference: SHE BLUSHED AND SMILED AND FUMBLED HIS CARD IN HER CONFUSION BEFORE SHE RAN UPSTAIRS
hypothesis: She blushed and smiled and fumbled his card in her confusion before she ran upstairs.

reference: ALEXANDER PACED UP AND DOWN THE HALLWAY BUTTONING AND UNBUTTONING HIS OVERCOAT UNTIL SHE RETURNED AND TOOK HIM UP TO HILDA'S LIVING ROOM
hypothesis: Alexander paced up and down the hallway buttoning and unbuttoning his overcoat until she returned and took him up to Hilda's living room.

reference: THE ROOM WAS EMPTY WHEN HE ENTERED
hypothesis: The room was empty when he entered.

reference: ALEXANDER DID NOT SIT DOWN
hypothesis: Alexander did not sit down.

reference: I FELT IT IN MY BONES WHEN I WOKE THIS MORNING THAT SOMETHING SPLENDID WAS GOING TO TURN UP
hypothesis: I felt it in my bones when I woke this morning that something splendid was going to turn up.

reference: I THOUGHT IT MIGHT BE SISTER KATE OR COUSIN MIKE WOULD BE HAPPENING ALONG
hypothesis: I thought it might be sister Kate or Cousin Mike would be happening along.

reference: SHE PUSHED HIM TOWARD THE BIG CHAIR BY THE FIRE AND SAT DOWN ON A STOOL AT THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF THE HEARTH HER KNEES DRAWN UP TO HER CHIN LAUGHING LIKE A HAPPY LITTLE GIRL
hypothesis: She pushed him toward the big chair by the fire and sat down on a stool at the opposite side of the hearth, her knees drawn up to her chin, laughing like a happy little girl.

reference: WHEN DID YOU COME BARTLEY AND HOW DID IT HAPPEN YOU HAVEN'T SPOKEN A WORD
hypothesis: When did you come, Bartley, and how did it happen? You haven't spoken a word.

reference: I GOT IN ABOUT TEN MINUTES AGO
hypothesis: I got in about 10 min ago.

reference: ALEXANDER LEANED FORWARD AND WARMED HIS HANDS BEFORE THE BLAZE
hypothesis: Alexander leaned forward and warmed his hands before the blaze.

reference: BARTLEY BENT LOWER OVER THE FIRE
hypothesis: Bartley bent lower over the fire.

reference: SHE LOOKED AT HIS HEAVY SHOULDERS AND BIG DETERMINED HEAD THRUST FORWARD LIKE A CATAPULT IN LEASH
hypothesis: She looked at his heavy shoulders and big determined head thrust forward like a catapult in leash.

reference: I'LL DO ANYTHING YOU WISH ME TO BARTLEY SHE SAID TREMULOUSLY
hypothesis: I'll do anything you wish me to, Bartley, she said tremulously.

reference: I CAN'T STAND SEEING YOU MISERABLE
hypothesis: I can't stand seeing you miserable.

reference: HE PULLED UP A WINDOW AS IF THE AIR WERE HEAVY
hypothesis: He pulled up a window as if the air were heavy.

reference: HILDA WATCHED HIM FROM HER CORNER TREMBLING AND SCARCELY BREATHING DARK SHADOWS GROWING ABOUT HER EYES IT
hypothesis: Hilda watched him from the corner, trembling and scarcely breathing, dark shadows growing about her eyes.

reference: BUT IT'S WORSE NOW IT'S UNBEARABLE
hypothesis: But it's worse now. It's unbearable.

reference: I GET NOTHING BUT MISERY OUT OF EITHER
hypothesis: I get nothing but misery out of either.

reference: THE WORLD IS ALL THERE JUST AS IT USED TO BE BUT I CAN'T GET AT IT ANY MORE
hypothesis: The world is all there just as it used to be, but I can't get at it anymore.

reference: IT WAS MYSELF I WAS DEFYING HILDA
hypothesis: It was myself. I was defying Hilda.

reference: HILDA'S FACE QUIVERED BUT SHE WHISPERED YES I THINK IT MUST HAVE BEEN
hypothesis: Hilda's face quivered, but she whispered, yes, I think it must have been.

reference: BUT WHY DIDN'T YOU TELL ME WHEN YOU WERE HERE IN THE SUMMER
hypothesis: But why didn't you tell me when you were here in the summer?

reference: ALEXANDER GROANED I MEANT TO BUT SOMEHOW I COULDN'T
hypothesis: Alexander groaned. I meant to, but somehow I couldn't.

reference: SHE PRESSED HIS HAND GENTLY IN GRATITUDE
hypothesis: She pressed his hand gently in gratitude.

reference: WEREN'T YOU HAPPY THEN AT ALL
hypothesis: Weren't you happy then at all?

reference: SHE CLOSED HER EYES AND TOOK A DEEP BREATH AS IF TO DRAW IN AGAIN THE FRAGRANCE OF THOSE DAYS
hypothesis: She closed her eyes and took a deep breath as if to draw in again the fragrance of those days.

reference: HE MOVED UNEASILY AND HIS CHAIR CREAKED
hypothesis: He moved uneasily and his chair creaked.

reference: YES YES SHE HURRIED PULLING HER HAND GENTLY AWAY FROM HIM
hypothesis: Yes, yes, she hurried, pulling her hand gently away from him.

reference: PLEASE TELL ME ONE THING BARTLEY AT LEAST TELL ME THAT YOU BELIEVE I THOUGHT I WAS MAKING YOU HAPPY
hypothesis: Please tell me one thing, Bartley. At least tell me that you believe I thought I was making you happy.

reference: YES HILDA I KNOW THAT HE SAID SIMPLY
hypothesis: Yes, Hilda, I know that, he said simply.

reference: I UNDERSTAND BARTLEY I WAS WRONG
hypothesis: I understand, Bartley. I was wrong.

reference: BUT I DIDN'T KNOW YOU'VE ONLY TO TELL ME NOW
hypothesis: But I didn't know. You've only to tell me now.

reference: WHAT I MEAN IS THAT I WANT YOU TO PROMISE NEVER TO SEE ME AGAIN NO MATTER HOW OFTEN I COME NO MATTER HOW HARD I BEG
hypothesis: What I mean is that I want you to promise never to see me again, no matter how often I come, no matter how hard I beg.

reference: KEEP AWAY IF YOU WISH WHEN HAVE I EVER FOLLOWED YOU
hypothesis: Keep away if you wish. When have I ever followed you?

reference: ALEXANDER ROSE AND SHOOK HIMSELF ANGRILY YES I KNOW I'M COWARDLY
hypothesis: Alexander rose and shook himself angrily. Yes, I know I'm cowardly.

reference: HE TOOK HER ROUGHLY IN HIS ARMS DO YOU KNOW WHAT I MEAN
hypothesis: He took her roughly in his arms. Do you know what I mean?

reference: OH BARTLEY WHAT AM I TO DO
hypothesis: Oh Bartley, what am I to do?

reference: I WILL ASK THE LEAST IMAGINABLE BUT I MUST HAVE SOMETHING
hypothesis: I will ask the least imaginable, but I must have something.

reference: I MUST KNOW ABOUT YOU
hypothesis: I must know about you.

reference: THE SIGHT OF YOU BARTLEY TO SEE YOU LIVING AND HAPPY AND SUCCESSFUL CAN I NEVER MAKE YOU UNDERSTAND WHAT THAT MEANS TO ME
hypothesis: The sight of you, Bartley, to see you living and happy and successful. Can I never make you understand what that means to me?

reference: YOU SEE LOVING SOME ONE AS I LOVE YOU MAKES THE WHOLE WORLD DIFFERENT
hypothesis: You see, loving someone as I love you makes the whole world different.

reference: AND THEN YOU CAME BACK NOT CARING VERY MUCH BUT IT MADE NO DIFFERENCE
hypothesis: And then you came back not caring very much, but it made no difference.

reference: BARTLEY BENT OVER AND TOOK HER IN HIS ARMS KISSING HER MOUTH AND HER WET TIRED EYES
hypothesis: Bartley bent over and took her in his arms, kissing her mouth and her wet, tired eyes.

reference: DON'T CRY DON'T CRY HE WHISPERED
hypothesis: Don't cry, don't cry, he whispered.

reference: WE'VE TORTURED EACH OTHER ENOUGH FOR TONIGHT
hypothesis: We've tortured each other enough for tonight.

reference: THE ARMY FOUND THE PEOPLE IN POVERTY AND LEFT THEM IN COMPARATIVE WEALTH
hypothesis: The army found the people in poverty and left them in comparative wealth.

reference: BUT A WORD FURTHER CONCERNING THE EXPEDITION IN GENERAL
hypothesis: But a word further concerning the expedition in general.

reference: IT WAS THROUGH FLOYD'S ADVICE THAT BUCHANAN ORDERED THE MILITARY EXPEDITION TO UTAH OSTENSIBLY TO INSTALL CERTAIN FEDERAL OFFICIALS AND TO REPRESS AN ALLEGED INFANTILE REBELLION WHICH IN FACT HAD NEVER COME INTO EXISTENCE BUT IN REALITY TO FURTHER THE INTERESTS OF THE SECESSIONISTS
hypothesis: It was through Floyd's advice that Buchanan ordered the military expedition to Utah, ostensibly to install certain federal officials and to repress an alleged infantile rebellion which in fact had never come into existence, but in reality to further the interests of the secessionists.

reference: MOREOVER HAD THE PEOPLE BEEN INCLINED TO REBELLION WHAT GREATER OPPORTUNITY COULD THEY HAVE WISHED
hypothesis: Moreover, had the people been inclined to rebellion, greater opportunity could they have wished.

reference: ALREADY A NORTH AND A SOUTH WERE TALKED OF WHY NOT SET UP ALSO A WEST
hypothesis: Already a North and a South were talked of. Why not set up also a West?

reference: THEY KNEW NO NORTH NO SOUTH NO EAST NO WEST THEY STOOD POSITIVELY BY THE CONSTITUTION AND WOULD HAVE NOTHING TO DO IN THE BLOODY STRIFE BETWEEN BROTHERS UNLESS INDEED THEY WERE SUMMONED BY THE AUTHORITY TO WHICH THEY HAD ALREADY ONCE LOYALLY RESPONDED TO FURNISH MEN AND ARMS FOR THEIR COUNTRY'S NEED
hypothesis: They knew no North, no South, no East, no West. They stood positively by the Constitution and would have nothing to do in the bloody strife between brothers, unless indeed they were summoned by the authority to which they had already once loyally responded to furnish men and arms for the country's need.

reference: WHAT THE LATTER DAY SAINTS CALL CELESTIAL MARRIAGE IS CHARACTERISTIC OF THE CHURCH AND IS IN VERY GENERAL PRACTISE BUT OF CELESTIAL MARRIAGE PLURALITY OF WIVES WAS AN INCIDENT NEVER AN ESSENTIAL
hypothesis: What the Latter day Saints call celestial marriage is characteristic of the church and is in very general practice. But of celestial marriage, plurality of wives was an incident, never an essential.

reference: WE BELIEVE IN A LITERAL RESURRECTION AND AN ACTUAL HEREAFTER IN WHICH FUTURE STATE SHALL BE RECOGNIZED EVERY SANCTIFIED AND AUTHORIZED RELATIONSHIP EXISTING HERE ON EARTH OF PARENT AND CHILD BROTHER AND SISTER HUSBAND AND WIFE
hypothesis: We believe in a literal resurrection and an actual hereafter in which future states shall be recognized every sanctified and authorized relationship existing here on earth of parent and child, brother and sister, husband and wife.

reference: IT HAS BEEN MY PRIVILEGE TO TREAD THE SOIL OF MANY LANDS TO OBSERVE THE CUSTOMS AND STUDY THE HABITS OF MORE NATIONS THAN ONE AND I HAVE YET TO FIND THE PLACE AND MEET THE PEOPLE WHERE AND WITH WHOM THE PURITY OF MAN AND WOMAN IS HELD MORE PRECIOUS THAN AMONG THE MALIGNED MORMONS IN THE MOUNTAIN VALLEYS OF THE WEST
hypothesis: It has been my privilege to tread the soil of many lands, to observe the customs and study the habits of more nations than one.

reference: AT THE INCEPTION OF PLURAL MARRIAGE AMONG THE LATTER DAY SAINTS THERE WAS NO LAW NATIONAL OR STATE AGAINST ITS PRACTISE
hypothesis: At the inception of plural marriage among the Latter day Saints, there was no law, national or state, against its practice.

reference: IN EIGHTEEN SIXTY TWO A LAW WAS ENACTED WITH THE PURPOSE OF SUPPRESSING PLURAL MARRIAGE AND AS HAD BEEN PREDICTED IN THE NATIONAL SENATE PRIOR TO ITS PASSAGE IT LAY FOR MANY YEARS A DEAD LETTER
hypothesis: In 1862 a law was enacted with for the purpose of suppressing plural marriage, and as had been predicted in the National Senate prior to its passage, it lay for many years a dead letter.

reference: FEDERAL JUDGES AND UNITED STATES ATTORNEYS IN UTAH WHO WERE NOT MORMONS NOR LOVERS OF MORMONISM REFUSED TO ENTERTAIN COMPLAINTS OR PROSECUTE CASES UNDER THE LAW BECAUSE OF ITS MANIFEST INJUSTICE AND INADEQUACY
hypothesis: Federal judges and United States attorneys in Utah who were not Mormons nor lovers of Mormonism refused to entertain complaints or prosecute cases under the law because of its manifest injustice and inadequacy.

reference: THIS MEANT THAT FOR AN ALLEGED MISDEMEANOR FOR WHICH CONGRESS PRESCRIBED A MAXIMUM PENALTY OF SIX MONTHS IMPRISONMENT AND A FINE OF THREE HUNDRED DOLLARS A MAN MIGHT BE IMPRISONED FOR LIFE AYE FOR MANY TERMS OF A MAN'S NATURAL LIFE DID THE COURT'S POWER TO ENFORCE ITS SENTENCES EXTEND SO FAR AND MIGHT BE FINED MILLIONS OF DOLLARS
hypothesis: This meant that for an alleged misdemeanor for which Congress prescribed a maximum penalty of six months imprisonment and a fine of $300, a man might be imprisoned for life, ay, for many terms of a man's natural life. did the court's power to enforce its sentences extend so far and might be fined millions of dollars.

reference: BEFORE THIS TRAVESTY ON THE ADMINISTRATION OF LAW COULD BE BROUGHT BEFORE THE COURT OF LAST RESORT AND THERE MEET WITH THE REVERSAL AND REBUKE IT DESERVED MEN WERE IMPRISONED UNDER SENTENCES OF MANY YEARS DURATION
hypothesis: Before this travesty on the administration of law could be brought before the court of last resort and there met with the reversal and rebuke it deserved, men were imprisoned under sentence of many years duration.

reference: THE PEOPLE CONTESTED THESE MEASURES ONE BY ONE IN THE COURTS PRESENTING IN CASE AFTER CASE THE DIFFERENT PHASES OF THE SUBJECT AND URGING THE UNCONSTITUTIONALITY OF THE MEASURE
hypothesis: The people contested these measures one by one in the courts, presenting in case after case the different phases of the subject and urging the unconstitutionality of the measure.

reference: THEN THE CHURCH WAS DISINCORPORATED AND ITS PROPERTY BOTH REAL AND PERSONAL CONFISCATED AND ESCHEATED TO THE GOVERNMENT OF THE UNITED STATES AND ALTHOUGH THE PERSONAL PROPERTY WAS SOON RESTORED REAL ESTATE OF GREAT VALUE LONG LAY IN THE HANDS OF THE COURT'S RECEIVER AND THE MORMON CHURCH HAD TO PAY THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT HIGH RENTAL ON ITS OWN PROPERTY
hypothesis: Then the church was dis incorporated and its property, both real and personal, confiscated and Eti to the government of the United States.

reference: AND SO THE STORY OF MORMONISM RUNS ON ITS FINALE HAS NOT YET BEEN WRITTEN THE CURRENT PRESS PRESENTS CONTINUOUSLY NEW STAGES OF ITS PROGRESS NEW DEVELOPMENTS OF ITS PLAN
hypothesis: And so the story of Mormonism runs on. Its finale has not yet been written. The current press presents continuously new stages of its progress, new developments of its plan.

reference: ON THE SIXTH OF APRIL EIGHTEEN THIRTY THE CHURCH OF JESUS CHRIST OF LATTER DAY SAINTS WAS FORMALLY ORGANIZED AND THUS TOOK ON A LEGAL EXISTENCE
hypothesis: On 6 April 1830, the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter day Saints was formally organized and thus took on a legal existence.

reference: ITS ORIGIN WAS SMALL A GERM AN INSIGNIFICANT SEED HARDLY TO BE THOUGHT OF AS LIKELY TO AROUSE OPPOSITION
hypothesis: Its origin was small, a germ, an insignificant seed, hardly to be thought of as likely to arouse opposition.

reference: INSTEAD OF BUT SIX REGULARLY AFFILIATED MEMBERS AND AT MOST TWO SCORE OF ADHERENTS THE ORGANIZATION NUMBERS TODAY MANY HUNDRED THOUSAND SOULS
hypothesis: Instead of but six regularly affiliated members and at most two score of adherents, the organization numbers today many hundred thousand souls.

reference: IN PLACE OF A SINGLE HAMLET IN THE SMALLEST CORNER OF WHICH THE MEMBERS COULD HAVE CONGREGATED THERE NOW ARE ABOUT SEVENTY STAKES OF ZION AND ABOUT SEVEN HUNDRED ORGANIZED WARDS EACH WARD AND STAKE WITH ITS FULL COMPLEMENT OF OFFICERS AND PRIESTHOOD ORGANIZATIONS
hypothesis: In place of a single hamlet in the smallest corner of which the members could have congregated, there now are about 70 stakes of Zion and about 700 organized wards, each ward and stake with its full complement of officers and priesthood organizations.

reference: THE PRACTISE OF GATHERING ITS PROSELYTES INTO ONE PLACE PREVENTS THE BUILDING UP AND STRENGTHENING OF FOREIGN BRANCHES AND INASMUCH AS EXTENSIVE AND STRONG ORGANIZATIONS ARE SELDOM MET WITH ABROAD VERY ERRONEOUS IDEAS EXIST CONCERNING THE STRENGTH OF THE CHURCH
hypothesis: The practice of gathering its proselytes into one place prevents the building up and strengthening of foreign branches. And inasmuch as extensive and strong organizations are seldom met with abroad, very erroneous ideas exist concerning the strength of the church.

reference: NEVERTHELESS THE MUSTARD SEED AMONG THE SMALLEST OF ALL SEEDS HAS ATTAINED THE PROPORTIONS OF A TREE AND THE BIRDS OF THE AIR ARE NESTING IN ITS BRANCHES THE ACORN IS NOW AN OAK OFFERING PROTECTION AND THE SWEETS OF SATISFACTION TO EVERY EARNEST PILGRIM JOURNEYING ITS WAY FOR TRUTH
hypothesis: Nevertheless, the mustard seed, among the small of all seeds, has attained the proportions of a tree, and the birds of the air are nesting in its branches. The acorn is now an oak, offering protection and the sweets of satisfaction to every earnest pilgrim journeying its way for truth.

reference: THEIR EYES WERE FROM THE FIRST TURNED IN ANTICIPATION TOWARD THE EVENING SUN NOT MERELY THAT THE WORK OF PROSELYTING SHOULD BE CARRIED ON IN THE WEST BUT THAT THE HEADQUARTERS OF THE CHURCH SHOULD BE THERE ESTABLISHED
hypothesis: Their eyes were from the 1st turned in anticipation toward the evening sun, not merely that the work of proselyting should be carried on in the West, but that the headquarters of the church should be there established.

reference: THE BOOK OF MORMON HAD TAUGHT THE PEOPLE THE TRUE ORIGIN AND DESTINY OF THE AMERICAN INDIANS AND TOWARD THIS DARK SKINNED REMNANT OF A ONCE MIGHTY PEOPLE THE MISSIONARIES OF MORMONISM EARLY TURNED THEIR EYES AND WITH THEIR EYES WENT THEIR HEARTS AND THEIR HOPES
hypothesis: The Book of Mormon had taught the people the true origin and destiny of the American Indians. And toward this dark skinned remnant of a once mighty people the missionaries of Mormonism early turned their eyes, and with their eyes went their hearts and their hopes.

reference: IT IS NOTABLE THAT THE INDIAN TRIBES HAVE GENERALLY REGARDED THE RELIGION OF THE LATTER DAY SAINTS WITH FAVOR SEEING IN THE BOOK OF MORMON STRIKING AGREEMENT WITH THEIR OWN TRADITIONS
hypothesis: It is notable that the Indian tribes have generally regarded the religion of the Latter day Saints with favor, seeing in the Book of Mormon striking agreement with their own traditions.

reference: THE FIRST WELL ESTABLISHED SEAT OF THE CHURCH WAS IN THE PRETTY LITTLE TOWN OF KIRTLAND OHIO ALMOST WITHIN SIGHT OF LAKE ERIE AND HERE SOON ROSE THE FIRST TEMPLE OF MODERN TIMES
hypothesis: The 1st well established seat of the church was in the pretty little town of Kirtland, Ohio, almost within sight of Lake Erie.

reference: TO THE FERVENT LATTER DAY SAINT A TEMPLE IS NOT SIMPLY A CHURCH BUILDING A HOUSE FOR RELIGIOUS ASSEMBLY
hypothesis: To the fervent latter day St., a temple is not simply a church building, a house for religious assembly.

reference: SOON THOUSANDS OF CONVERTS HAD RENTED OR PURCHASED HOMES IN MISSOURI INDEPENDENCE JACKSON COUNTY BEING THEIR CENTER BUT FROM THE FIRST THEY WERE UNPOPULAR AMONG THE MISSOURIANS
hypothesis: Soon, thousands of converts had rented or purchased homes in Missouri.

reference: THE LIEUTENANT GOVERNOR LILBURN W BOGGS AFTERWARD GOVERNOR WAS A PRONOUNCED MORMON HATER AND THROUGHOUT THE PERIOD OF THE TROUBLES HE MANIFESTED SYMPATHY WITH THE PERSECUTORS
hypothesis: The Lieutenant Governor, Little Burn W. Boggs, afterward governor, was a pronounced Mormon hater, and throughout the period of the troubles he manifests sympathy with the persecutors.

reference: THEIR SUFFERINGS HAVE NEVER YET BEEN FITLY CHRONICLED BY HUMAN SCRIBE
hypothesis: Their sufferings have never yet been fitly chronicled by human scribe.

reference: MAKING THEIR WAY ACROSS THE RIVER MOST OF THE REFUGEES FOUND SHELTER AMONG THE MORE HOSPITABLE PEOPLE OF CLAY COUNTY AND AFTERWARD ESTABLISHED THEMSELVES IN CALDWELL COUNTY THEREIN FOUNDING THE CITY OF FAR WEST
hypothesis: Making their way across the river, most of the refugees found shelter among the more hospitable people of Clay County and afterward established themselves in Caldwell County, therein founding the city of Far West.

reference: A SMALL SETTLEMENT HAD BEEN FOUNDED BY MORMON FAMILIES ON SHOAL CREEK AND HERE ON THE THIRTIETH OF OCTOBER EIGHTEEN THIRTY EIGHT A COMPANY OF TWO HUNDRED AND FORTY FELL UPON THE HAPLESS SETTLERS AND BUTCHERED A SCORE
hypothesis: A small settlement had been founded by Mormon families on Shoal Creek, and here on 30 October 1838, a company of 240 fell upon the hapless settlers and butchered a score.

reference: BE IT SAID TO THE HONOR OF SOME OF THE OFFICERS ENTRUSTED WITH THE TERRIBLE COMMISSION THAT WHEN THEY LEARNED ITS TRUE SIGNIFICANCE THEY RESIGNED THEIR AUTHORITY RATHER THAN HAVE ANYTHING TO DO WITH WHAT THEY DESIGNATED A COLD BLOODED BUTCHERY
hypothesis: Be it said to the honor of some of the officers entrusted with the terrible commission.

reference: OH WHAT A RECORD TO READ WHAT A PICTURE TO GAZE UPON HOW AWFUL THE FACT
hypothesis: Oh, what a record to read. What a picture to gaze upon. How awful the fact.

reference: AMERICAN SCHOOL BOYS READ WITH EMOTIONS OF HORROR OF THE ALBIGENSES DRIVEN BEATEN AND KILLED WITH A PAPAL LEGATE DIRECTING THE BUTCHERY AND OF THE VAUDOIS HUNTED AND HOUNDED LIKE BEASTS AS THE EFFECT OF A ROYAL DECREE AND THEY YET SHALL READ IN THE HISTORY OF THEIR OWN COUNTRY OF SCENES AS TERRIBLE AS THESE IN THE EXHIBITION OF INJUSTICE AND INHUMAN HATE
hypothesis: American schoolboys read with emotions of horror of the Albigenses driven, beaten and killed, with a papal legate directing the butchery, and of the Vaudois hunted and hounded like beasts as the effect of a royal decree.

reference: WHO BEGAN THE QUARREL WAS IT THE MORMONS
hypothesis: Who began the quarrel? Was it the Mormons?

reference: AS A SAMPLE OF THE PRESS COMMENTS AGAINST THE BRUTALITY OF THE MISSOURIANS I QUOTE A PARAGRAPH FROM THE QUINCY ARGUS MARCH SIXTEENTH EIGHTEEN THIRTY NINE
hypothesis: As a sample of the press comments against the brutality of the Missourians, I quote a paragraph from the Quincy Argus, March 16, 1839.

reference: IT WILL BE OBSERVED THAT AN ORGANIZED MOB AIDED BY MANY OF THE CIVIL AND MILITARY OFFICERS OF MISSOURI WITH GOVERNOR BOGGS AT THEIR HEAD HAVE BEEN THE PROMINENT ACTORS IN THIS BUSINESS INCITED TOO IT APPEARS AGAINST THE MORMONS BY POLITICAL HATRED AND BY THE ADDITIONAL MOTIVES OF PLUNDER AND REVENGE
hypothesis: It will be observed that an organized mob, aided by many of the civil and military officers of Missouri, with Governor Boggs at their head, have been the prominent actors in this business, incited too, it appears, against the Mormons by political hatred and by the additional motives of plunder and revenge.

reference: HESTER PRYNNE WENT ONE DAY TO THE MANSION OF GOVERNOR BELLINGHAM WITH A PAIR OF GLOVES WHICH SHE HAD FRINGED AND EMBROIDERED TO HIS ORDER AND WHICH WERE TO BE WORN ON SOME GREAT OCCASION OF STATE FOR THOUGH THE CHANCES OF A POPULAR ELECTION HAD CAUSED THIS FORMER RULER TO DESCEND A STEP OR TWO FROM THE HIGHEST RANK HE STILL HELD AN HONOURABLE AND INFLUENTIAL PLACE AMONG THE COLONIAL MAGISTRACY
hypothesis: Hester Prynne went one day to the mansion of Governor Bellingham with a pair of gloves which she had fringed and embroidered to his order, and which were to be worn on some great occasion of state.

reference: ANOTHER AND FAR MORE IMPORTANT REASON THAN THE DELIVERY OF A PAIR OF EMBROIDERED GLOVES IMPELLED HESTER AT THIS TIME TO SEEK AN INTERVIEW WITH A PERSONAGE OF SO MUCH POWER AND ACTIVITY IN THE AFFAIRS OF THE SETTLEMENT
hypothesis: Another and far more important reason than the delivery of a pair of embroidered gloves impelled Hester at this time to seek an interview with a personage of so much power and activity in the affairs of the settlement.

reference: AT THAT EPOCH OF PRISTINE SIMPLICITY HOWEVER MATTERS OF EVEN SLIGHTER PUBLIC INTEREST AND OF FAR LESS INTRINSIC WEIGHT THAN THE WELFARE OF HESTER AND HER CHILD WERE STRANGELY MIXED UP WITH THE DELIBERATIONS OF LEGISLATORS AND ACTS OF STATE
hypothesis: At that epoch of pristine simplicity, however, matters of even slighter public interest and of far less intrinsic weight than the welfare of Hester and her child were strangely mixed up with the deliberations of legislators and acts of state.

reference: THE PERIOD WAS HARDLY IF AT ALL EARLIER THAN THAT OF OUR STORY WHEN A DISPUTE CONCERNING THE RIGHT OF PROPERTY IN A PIG NOT ONLY CAUSED A FIERCE AND BITTER CONTEST IN THE LEGISLATIVE BODY OF THE COLONY BUT RESULTED IN AN IMPORTANT MODIFICATION OF THE FRAMEWORK ITSELF OF THE LEGISLATURE
hypothesis: The period was hardly, if at all, earlier than that of our story, when a dispute concerning the right of property in a pig not only caused a fierce and bitter contest in the legislative body of the colony, but resulted in an important modification of the framework itself of the legislature.

reference: WE HAVE SPOKEN OF PEARL'S RICH AND LUXURIANT BEAUTY A BEAUTY THAT SHONE WITH DEEP AND VIVID TINTS A BRIGHT COMPLEXION EYES POSSESSING INTENSITY BOTH OF DEPTH AND GLOW AND HAIR ALREADY OF A DEEP GLOSSY BROWN AND WHICH IN AFTER YEARS WOULD BE NEARLY AKIN TO BLACK
hypothesis: We have spoken of pearls, rich and luxuriant beauty, a beauty that shone with deep and vivid tints, a bright complexion, eyes possessing intensity both of depth and glow, and hair already of a deep glossy brown, and which in after years would be nearly akin to black.

reference: IT WAS THE SCARLET LETTER IN ANOTHER FORM THE SCARLET LETTER ENDOWED WITH LIFE
hypothesis: It was the scarlet letter in another form, the scarlet letter endowed with life.

reference: THE MOTHER HERSELF AS IF THE RED IGNOMINY WERE SO DEEPLY SCORCHED INTO HER BRAIN THAT ALL HER CONCEPTIONS ASSUMED ITS FORM HAD CAREFULLY WROUGHT OUT THE SIMILITUDE LAVISHING MANY HOURS OF MORBID INGENUITY TO CREATE AN ANALOGY BETWEEN THE OBJECT OF HER AFFECTION AND THE EMBLEM OF HER GUILT AND TORTURE
hypothesis: The mother herself, as if the red ignominy were so deeply scorched into her brain that all her conceptions assumed its form, had carefully wrought out the similitude, lavishing many hours of morbid ingenuity to create an analogy between the object of her affection and the emblem of her guilt and torture.

reference: BUT IN TRUTH PEARL WAS THE ONE AS WELL AS THE OTHER AND ONLY IN CONSEQUENCE OF THAT IDENTITY HAD HESTER CONTRIVED SO PERFECTLY TO REPRESENT THE SCARLET LETTER IN HER APPEARANCE
hypothesis: But in truth, Pearl was the one as well as the other, and only in consequence of that identity had Hester contrived so perfectly to represent the scarlet letter in her appearance.

reference: COME THEREFORE AND LET US FLING MUD AT THEM
hypothesis: Come therefore and let us fling mud at them.

reference: BUT PEARL WHO WAS A DAUNTLESS CHILD AFTER FROWNING STAMPING HER FOOT AND SHAKING HER LITTLE HAND WITH A VARIETY OF THREATENING GESTURES SUDDENLY MADE A RUSH AT THE KNOT OF HER ENEMIES AND PUT THEM ALL TO FLIGHT
hypothesis: But Pearl, who was a dauntless child, after frowning, stamping her foot, and shaking her little hand with a variety of threatening gestures, suddenly made a rush at the knot of her enemies and put them all to flight.

reference: SHE SCREAMED AND SHOUTED TOO WITH A TERRIFIC VOLUME OF SOUND WHICH DOUBTLESS CAUSED THE HEARTS OF THE FUGITIVES TO QUAKE WITHIN THEM
hypothesis: She screamed and shouted too, with a terrific volume of sound, which doubtless caused the hearts of the fugitives to quake within them.

reference: IT WAS FURTHER DECORATED WITH STRANGE AND SEEMINGLY CABALISTIC FIGURES AND DIAGRAMS SUITABLE TO THE QUAINT TASTE OF THE AGE WHICH HAD BEEN DRAWN IN THE STUCCO WHEN NEWLY LAID ON AND HAD NOW GROWN HARD AND DURABLE FOR THE ADMIRATION OF AFTER TIMES
hypothesis: It was further decorated with strange and seemingly Kabbalistic figures and diagrams suitable to the quaint taste of the age, which had been drawn in the stucco when newly laid on, and had now grown hard and durable for the admiration of after times.

reference: THEY APPROACHED THE DOOR WHICH WAS OF AN ARCHED FORM AND FLANKED ON EACH SIDE BY A NARROW TOWER OR PROJECTION OF THE EDIFICE IN BOTH OF WHICH WERE LATTICE WINDOWS THE WOODEN SHUTTERS TO CLOSE OVER THEM AT NEED
hypothesis: They approached the door, which was of an arched form and flanked on each side by a narrow tower or projection of the edifice, in both of which were lattice windows, the wooden shutters to close over them at need.

reference: LIFTING THE IRON HAMMER THAT HUNG AT THE PORTAL HESTER PRYNNE GAVE A SUMMONS WHICH WAS ANSWERED BY ONE OF THE GOVERNOR'S BOND SERVANT A FREE BORN ENGLISHMAN BUT NOW A SEVEN YEARS SLAVE
hypothesis: Lifting the iron hammer that hung at the portal, Hester Prynne gave a summons, which was answered by one of the governor's bond servants, a free born Englishman, but now a seven years slave.

reference: YEA HIS HONOURABLE WORSHIP IS WITHIN BUT HE HATH A GODLY MINISTER OR TWO WITH HIM AND LIKEWISE A LEECH
hypothesis: Yea, his honourable worship is within, but he hath a godly minister or two with him, and likewise a leech.

reference: YE MAY NOT SEE HIS WORSHIP NOW
hypothesis: Ye may not see his worship now.

reference: WITH MANY VARIATIONS SUGGESTED BY THE NATURE OF HIS BUILDING MATERIALS DIVERSITY OF CLIMATE AND A DIFFERENT MODE OF SOCIAL LIFE GOVERNOR BELLINGHAM HAD PLANNED HIS NEW HABITATION AFTER THE RESIDENCES OF GENTLEMEN OF FAIR ESTATE IN HIS NATIVE LAND
hypothesis: With many variations suggested by the nature of his building materials, diversity of climate, and a different mode of social life, Governor Bellingham had planned his new habitation after the residences of gentlemen of fair estate in his native land.

reference: ON THE TABLE IN TOKEN THAT THE SENTIMENT OF OLD ENGLISH HOSPITALITY HAD NOT BEEN LEFT BEHIND STOOD A LARGE PEWTER TANKARD AT THE BOTTOM OF WHICH HAD HESTER OR PEARL PEEPED INTO IT THEY MIGHT HAVE SEEN THE FROTHY REMNANT OF A RECENT DRAUGHT OF ALE
hypothesis: On the table in token that the sentiment of old English hospitality had not been left behind.

reference: LITTLE PEARL WHO WAS AS GREATLY PLEASED WITH THE GLEAMING ARMOUR AS SHE HAD BEEN WITH THE GLITTERING FRONTISPIECE OF THE HOUSE SPENT SOME TIME LOOKING INTO THE POLISHED MIRROR OF THE BREASTPLATE
hypothesis: Little Pearl, who was as greatly pleased with the gleaming armor as she had been with the glittering frontispiece of the house, spent some time looking into the polished mirror of the breastplate.

reference: MOTHER CRIED SHE I SEE YOU HERE LOOK LOOK
hypothesis: Mother, cried she, I see you here. Look, look.

reference: IN TRUTH SHE SEEMED ABSOLUTELY HIDDEN BEHIND IT
hypothesis: In truth, she seemed absolutely hidden behind it.

reference: PEARL ACCORDINGLY RAN TO THE BOW WINDOW AT THE FURTHER END OF THE HALL AND LOOKED ALONG THE VISTA OF A GARDEN WALK CARPETED WITH CLOSELY SHAVEN GRASS AND BORDERED WITH SOME RUDE AND IMMATURE ATTEMPT AT SHRUBBERY
hypothesis: Pearl accordingly ran to the bow window at the further end of the hall and looked along the vista of a garden walk carpeted with closely shaven grass and bordered with some rude and immature attempt at shrubbery.

reference: BUT THE PROPRIETOR APPEARED ALREADY TO HAVE RELINQUISHED AS HOPELESS THE EFFORT TO PERPETUATE ON THIS SIDE OF THE ATLANTIC IN A HARD SOIL AND AMID THE CLOSE STRUGGLE FOR SUBSISTENCE THE NATIVE ENGLISH TASTE FOR ORNAMENTAL GARDENING
hypothesis: But the proprietor appeared already to have relinquished as hopeless the effort to perpetuate on this side of the Atlantic, in a hard soil and amid the close struggle for subsistence, the native English taste for ornamental gardening.

reference: THERE WERE A FEW ROSE BUSHES HOWEVER AND A NUMBER OF APPLE TREES PROBABLY THE DESCENDANTS OF THOSE PLANTED BY THE REVEREND MISTER BLACKSTONE THE FIRST SETTLER OF THE PENINSULA THAT HALF MYTHOLOGICAL PERSONAGE WHO RIDES THROUGH OUR EARLY ANNALS SEATED ON THE BACK OF A BULL
hypothesis: There were a few rose bushes, however, and a number of apple trees, probably the descendants of those planted by the Rev. Mr. Blackstone, the 1st settler of the peninsula, that half mythological personage who rides through our early annals seated on the back of a bull.

reference: PEARL SEEING THE ROSE BUSHES BEGAN TO CRY FOR A RED ROSE AND WOULD NOT BE PACIFIED
hypothesis: Pearl, seeing the rose bushes, began to cry for Red Rose and would not be pacified.

reference: HOW STRANGE IT SEEMED TO THE SAD WOMAN AS SHE WATCHED THE GROWTH AND THE BEAUTY THAT BECAME EVERY DAY MORE BRILLIANT AND THE INTELLIGENCE THAT THREW ITS QUIVERING SUNSHINE OVER THE TINY FEATURES OF THIS CHILD
hypothesis: How strange it seemed to the sad woman as she watched the growth and the beauty that became every day more brilliant, and the intelligence that threw its quivering sunshine over the tiny features of this child.

reference: GOD AS A DIRECT CONSEQUENCE OF THE SIN WHICH MAN THUS PUNISHED HAD GIVEN HER A LOVELY CHILD WHOSE PLACE WAS ON THAT SAME DISHONOURED BOSOM TO CONNECT HER PARENT FOR EVER WITH THE RACE AND DESCENT OF MORTALS AND TO BE FINALLY A BLESSED SOUL IN HEAVEN
hypothesis: God, as a direct consequence of the sin which man thus punished, had given her a lovely child whose place was on that same dishonoured bosom, to connect her parent forever with the race and descent of mortals, and to be finally a blessed soul in heaven.

reference: YET THESE THOUGHTS AFFECTED HESTER PRYNNE LESS WITH HOPE THAN APPREHENSION
hypothesis: Yet these thoughts affected Hester Prynne less with hope than apprehension.

reference: THE CHILD HAD A NATIVE GRACE WHICH DOES NOT INVARIABLY CO EXIST WITH FAULTLESS BEAUTY ITS ATTIRE HOWEVER SIMPLE ALWAYS IMPRESSED THE BEHOLDER AS IF IT WERE THE VERY GARB THAT PRECISELY BECAME IT BEST
hypothesis: The child had a native grace which does not invariably coexist with faultless beauty. Its attire, however simple, always impressed the beholder as if it were the very garb that precisely became it best.

reference: THIS OUTWARD MUTABILITY INDICATED AND DID NOT MORE THAN FAIRLY EXPRESS THE VARIOUS PROPERTIES OF HER INNER LIFE
hypothesis: This outward mutability indicated and did not more than fairly express the various properties of her inner life.

reference: HESTER COULD ONLY ACCOUNT FOR THE CHILD'S CHARACTER AND EVEN THEN MOST VAGUELY AND IMPERFECTLY BY RECALLING WHAT SHE HERSELF HAD BEEN DURING THAT MOMENTOUS PERIOD WHILE PEARL WAS IMBIBING HER SOUL FROM THE SPIRITUAL WORLD AND HER BODILY FRAME FROM ITS MATERIAL OF EARTH
hypothesis: Hester could only account for the child's character, and even then most vaguely and imperfectly, by recalling what she herself had been during that momentous period while Pearl was imbibing her soul from the spiritual world and her bodily frame from its material of earth.

reference: THEY WERE NOW ILLUMINATED BY THE MORNING RADIANCE OF A YOUNG CHILD'S DISPOSITION BUT LATER IN THE DAY OF EARTHLY EXISTENCE MIGHT BE PROLIFIC OF THE STORM AND WHIRLWIND
hypothesis: They were now illuminated by the morning radiance of a young child's disposition, but later in the day of earthly existence might be prolific of the storm and whirlwind.

reference: HESTER PRYNNE NEVERTHELESS THE LOVING MOTHER OF THIS ONE CHILD RAN LITTLE RISK OF ERRING ON THE SIDE OF UNDUE SEVERITY
hypothesis: Hester Prynne, nevertheless, the loving mother of this one child, ran little risk of erring on the side of undue severity.

reference: MINDFUL HOWEVER OF HER OWN ERRORS AND MISFORTUNES SHE EARLY SOUGHT TO IMPOSE A TENDER BUT STRICT CONTROL OVER THE INFANT IMMORTALITY THAT WAS COMMITTED TO HER CHARGE
hypothesis: Mindful, however, of her own errors and misfortunes, she early sought to impose a tender but strict control over the infant immortality that was committed to her charge.

reference: AS TO ANY OTHER KIND OF DISCIPLINE WHETHER ADDRESSED TO HER MIND OR HEART LITTLE PEARL MIGHT OR MIGHT NOT BE WITHIN ITS REACH IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CAPRICE THAT RULED THE MOMENT
hypothesis: As to any other kind of discipline, whether addressed to her mind or heart, little Pearl might or might not be within its reach in accordance with the caprice that ruled the moment.

reference: IT WAS A LOOK SO INTELLIGENT YET INEXPLICABLE PERVERSE SOMETIMES SO MALICIOUS BUT GENERALLY ACCOMPANIED BY A WILD FLOW OF SPIRITS THAT HESTER COULD NOT HELP QUESTIONING AT SUCH MOMENTS WHETHER PEARL WAS A HUMAN CHILD
hypothesis: It was a look so intelligent, yet inexplicable, perverse, sometimes so malicious, but generally accompanied by a wild flow of spirits, that Hester could not help questioning at such moments whether Pearl was a human child.

reference: BEHOLDING IT HESTER WAS CONSTRAINED TO RUSH TOWARDS THE CHILD TO PURSUE THE LITTLE ELF IN THE FLIGHT WHICH SHE INVARIABLY BEGAN TO SNATCH HER TO HER BOSOM WITH A CLOSE PRESSURE AND EARNEST KISSES NOT SO MUCH FROM OVERFLOWING LOVE AS TO ASSURE HERSELF THAT PEARL WAS FLESH AND BLOOD AND NOT UTTERLY DELUSIVE
hypothesis: Beholding it, Hester was constrained to rush towards the child, to pursue the little elf in the flight which she invariably began, to snatch her to her bosom with a close pressure and earnest kisses, not so much from overflowing love as to assure herself that Pearl was flesh and blood and not utterly delusive.

reference: BROODING OVER ALL THESE MATTERS THE MOTHER FELT LIKE ONE WHO HAS EVOKED A SPIRIT BUT BY SOME IRREGULARITY IN THE PROCESS OF CONJURATION HAS FAILED TO WIN THE MASTER WORD THAT SHOULD CONTROL THIS NEW AND INCOMPREHENSIBLE INTELLIGENCE
hypothesis: Brooding over all these matters, the mother felt like one who has evoked a spirit, but by some irregularity in the process of conjuration has failed to win the master word that should control this new and incomprehensible intelligence.

reference: PEARL WAS A BORN OUTCAST OF THE INFANTILE WORLD
hypothesis: Pearl was a born outcast of the infantile world.

reference: PEARL SAW AND GAZED INTENTLY BUT NEVER SOUGHT TO MAKE ACQUAINTANCE
hypothesis: Pearl saw and gazed intently, but never sought to make acquaintance.

reference: IF SPOKEN TO SHE WOULD NOT SPEAK AGAIN
hypothesis: If spoken to, she would not speak again.

reference: TO FADE AWAY LIKE MORNING BEAUTY FROM HER MORTAL DAY DOWN BY THE RIVER OF ADONA HER SOFT VOICE IS HEARD AND THUS HER GENTLE LAMENTATION FALLS LIKE MORNING DEW
hypothesis: To fade away like morning beauty from her mortal day.

reference: O LIFE OF THIS OUR SPRING
hypothesis: O life of this a spring.

reference: WHY FADES THE LOTUS OF THE WATER
hypothesis: Why fades the lotus of the water?

reference: WHY FADE THESE CHILDREN OF THE SPRING
hypothesis: Why fade these children of the spring?

hypothesis: Fell is like a watery bow and like a parting cloud.

reference: LIKE THE DOVES VOICE LIKE TRANSIENT DAY LIKE MUSIC IN THE AIR AH
hypothesis: Like the dove's voice, like transient day, like music in the air. Ah.

reference: AND GENTLE SLEEP THE SLEEP OF DEATH AND GENTLY HEAR THE VOICE OF HIM THAT WALKETH IN THE GARDEN IN THE EVENING TIME
hypothesis: And gentle sleep, the sleep of death, and gently hear the voice of him that walketh in the garden in the evening time.

reference: THE LILLY OF THE VALLEY BREATHING IN THE HUMBLE GRASS ANSWERD THE LOVELY MAID AND SAID I AM A WATRY WEED AND I AM VERY SMALL AND LOVE TO DWELL IN LOWLY VALES SO WEAK THE GILDED BUTTERFLY SCARCE PERCHES ON MY HEAD YET I AM VISITED FROM HEAVEN AND HE THAT SMILES ON ALL WALKS IN THE VALLEY AND EACH MORN OVER ME SPREADS HIS HAND SAYING REJOICE THOU HUMBLE GRASS THOU NEW BORN LILY FLOWER
hypothesis: The lily of the valley, breathing in the humble grass, Answered the lovely maid and said, I am a water weed, and I am very small, And love to dwell in lowly vales so weak. The gilded butterfly scarce perches on my head, Yet I am visited from heaven. And he that smiles on all walks in the valley, and each morn over me spreads his hand, saying, Rejoice, thou humble grass, thou newborn lily flower.

reference: THOU GENTLE MAID OF SILENT VALLEYS AND OF MODEST BROOKS FOR THOU SHALL BE CLOTHED IN LIGHT AND FED WITH MORNING MANNA TILL SUMMERS HEAT MELTS THEE BESIDE THE FOUNTAINS AND THE SPRINGS TO FLOURISH IN ETERNAL VALES THEY WHY SHOULD THEL COMPLAIN
hypothesis: Thou gentle maid of silent valleys and of modest brooks, For thou shall be clothed in light and fed with morning manna till summer's heat Melts thee beside the fountains and the springs To flourish in eternal vales. They. why should thou complain?

reference: WHY SHOULD THE MISTRESS OF THE VALES OF HAR UTTER A SIGH
hypothesis: Why should the mistress of the vales of Har utter a sigh?

reference: SHE CEASD AND SMILD IN TEARS THEN SAT DOWN IN HER SILVER SHRINE
hypothesis: She ceased and smiled in tears, then sat down in her silver shrine.

reference: WHICH THOU DOST SCATTER ON EVERY LITTLE BLADE OF GRASS THAT SPRINGS REVIVES THE MILKED COW AND TAMES THE FIRE BREATHING STEED
hypothesis: Which thou dost scatter on every little blade of grass that springs, Revives the milked cow and tames the fire breathing steed.

reference: BUT THEL IS LIKE A FAINT CLOUD KINDLED AT THE RISING SUN I VANISH FROM MY PEARLY THRONE AND WHO SHALL FIND MY PLACE
hypothesis: But thou is like a faint cloud.

reference: AND WHY IT SCATTERS ITS BRIGHT BEAUTY THRO THE HUMID AIR
hypothesis: And why it scatters its bright beauty through the humid air.

reference: DESCEND O LITTLE CLOUD AND HOVER BEFORE THE EYES OF THEL
hypothesis: Descend, O little cloud, and hover before the eyes of Fell.

hypothesis: O little cloud, the Virgin said, I charge thee to tell me why thou complaint now when in 1 H thou fade away, then we shall seek thee but not find. ah, fell is like to thee.

reference: I PASS AWAY YET I COMPLAIN AND NO ONE HEARS MY VOICE
hypothesis: I pass away, yet I complain and no one hears my voice.

reference: THE CLOUD THEN SHEWD HIS GOLDEN HEAD AND HIS BRIGHT FORM EMERG'D
hypothesis: The cloud then showed his golden head and his bright form emerged.

reference: AND FEAREST THOU BECAUSE I VANISH AND AM SEEN NO MORE
hypothesis: And fearest thou because I vanish and am seen no more?

reference: IT IS TO TENFOLD LIFE TO LOVE TO PEACE AND RAPTURES HOLY UNSEEN DESCENDING WEIGH MY LIGHT WINGS UPON BALMY FLOWERS AND COURT THE FAIR EYED DEW TO TAKE ME TO HER SHINING TENT THE WEEPING VIRGIN TREMBLING KNEELS BEFORE THE RISEN SUN
hypothesis: It is to tenfold life, to love, to peace and raptures. holy.

reference: TILL WE ARISE LINK'D IN A GOLDEN BAND AND NEVER PART BUT WALK UNITED BEARING FOOD TO ALL OUR TENDER FLOWERS
hypothesis: Till we arise linked in a golden band, And never part, but walk united, bearing food to all our tender flowers.

reference: LIVES NOT ALONE NOR OR ITSELF FEAR NOT AND I WILL CALL THE WEAK WORM FROM ITS LOWLY BED AND THOU SHALT HEAR ITS VOICE
hypothesis: Lives not alone nor of itself.

reference: COME FORTH WORM AND THE SILENT VALLEY TO THY PENSIVE QUEEN
hypothesis: Come forth, worm, and the silent valley to thy pensive queen.

reference: THE HELPLESS WORM AROSE AND SAT UPON THE LILLYS LEAF AND THE BRIGHT CLOUD SAILD ON TO FIND HIS PARTNER IN THE VALE
hypothesis: The helpless worm arose and sat upon the lily's leaf, And the bright cloud sailed on to find his partner in the veil.

reference: IMAGE OF WEAKNESS ART THOU BUT A WORM
hypothesis: Image of weakness, art thou but a worm?

reference: I SEE THEY LAY HELPLESS AND NAKED WEEPING AND NONE TO ANSWER NONE TO CHERISH THEE WITH MOTHERS SMILES
hypothesis: I see they lay helpless and naked, weeping, and none to answer, none to cherish thee with mother's smiles.

reference: AND SAYS THOU MOTHER OF MY CHILDREN I HAVE LOVED THEE AND I HAVE GIVEN THEE A CROWN THAT NONE CAN TAKE AWAY
hypothesis: And says, thou mother of my children, I have loved thee and I have given thee a crown that none can take away.

reference: AND LAY ME DOWN IN THY COLD BED AND LEAVE MY SHINING LOT
hypothesis: And lay me down in thy cold bed and leave my shining lot.

reference: OR AN EYE OF GIFTS AND GRACES SHOWRING FRUITS AND COINED GOLD
hypothesis: Or an eye of gifts and graces, showering fruits and coined gold.

reference: WHY A TONGUE IMPRESS'D WITH HONEY FROM EVERY WIND
hypothesis: Why a tongue impressed with honey from every wind.

reference: WHY AN EAR A WHIRLPOOL FIERCE TO DRAW CREATIONS IN
hypothesis: Why an ear, a whirlpool, fierce to draw creations in.

reference: ALL IS SAID WITHOUT A WORD
hypothesis: All is said without a word.

reference: I SIT BENEATH THY LOOKS AS CHILDREN DO IN THE NOON SUN WITH SOULS THAT TREMBLE THROUGH THEIR HAPPY EYELIDS FROM AN UNAVERRED YET PRODIGAL INWARD JOY
hypothesis: I sit beneath thy looks as children do in the sun, with souls that tremble through their happy eyelids from an un averred yet prodigal inward joy.

reference: I DID NOT WRONG MYSELF SO BUT I PLACED A WRONG ON THEE
hypothesis: I did not wrong myself so, but I placed a wrong on thee.

reference: WHEN CALLED BEFORE I TOLD HOW HASTILY I DROPPED MY FLOWERS OR BRAKE OFF FROM A GAME
hypothesis: When called before, I told how hastily I dropped my flowers or break off from a game.

reference: SHALL I NEVER MISS HOME TALK AND BLESSING AND THE COMMON KISS THAT COMES TO EACH IN TURN NOR COUNT IT STRANGE WHEN I LOOK UP TO DROP ON A NEW RANGE OF WALLS AND FLOORS ANOTHER HOME THAN THIS
hypothesis: Shall I never miss home talk and blessing and the common kiss that comes to each in turn, nor count it strange when I look up to drop on a new range of walls and floors another home than this?

reference: ALAS I HAVE GRIEVED SO I AM HARD TO LOVE
hypothesis: Alas.

reference: OPEN THY HEART WIDE AND FOLD WITHIN THE WET WINGS OF THY DOVE
hypothesis: Open thy heart wide and fold within the wet wings of thy dove.

reference: COULD IT MEAN TO LAST A LOVE SET PENDULOUS BETWEEN SORROW AND SORROW
hypothesis: Could it mean to last a love set pendulous between sorrow and sorrow?

reference: NAY I RATHER THRILLED DISTRUSTING EVERY LIGHT THAT SEEMED TO GILD THE ONWARD PATH AND FEARED TO OVERLEAN A FINGER EVEN
hypothesis: Nay, I rather thrilled, distrusting every light that seemed to gild the onward path, and feared to over lean a finger even.

reference: AND THOUGH I HAVE GROWN SERENE AND STRONG SINCE THEN I THINK THAT GOD HAS WILLED A STILL RENEWABLE FEAR
hypothesis: And though I have grown serene and strong since then, I think that God has willed a still renewable fear.

reference: O LOVE O TROTH
hypothesis: Oh love. Oh troth.

reference: AND LOVE BE FALSE
hypothesis: And love be false.

reference: IF HE TO KEEP ONE OATH MUST LOSE ONE JOY BY HIS LIFE'S STAR FORETOLD
hypothesis: If he to keep one oath must lose one joy by his life's star foretold.

reference: SLOW TO WORLD GREETINGS QUICK WITH ITS O LIST WHEN THE ANGELS SPEAK
hypothesis: Slow to world greetings, quick with its oh list when the angels speak.

reference: A RING OF AMETHYST I COULD NOT WEAR HERE PLAINER TO MY SIGHT THAN THAT FIRST KISS
hypothesis: A ring of amethyst I could not wear here plainer to my sight than that 1st kiss.

reference: THAT WAS THE CHRISM OF LOVE WHICH LOVE'S OWN CROWN WITH SANCTIFYING SWEETNESS DID PRECEDE THE THIRD UPON MY LIPS WAS FOLDED DOWN IN PERFECT PURPLE STATE SINCE WHEN INDEED I HAVE BEEN PROUD AND SAID MY LOVE MY OWN
hypothesis: That was the chrism of love, which love's own crown with sanctifying sweetness did proceed. The 3rd upon my lips was folded down in perfect purple state.

reference: DEAREST TEACH ME SO TO POUR OUT GRATITUDE AS THOU DOST GOOD
hypothesis: Dearest, teach me so to pour out gratitude as thou dost. Good.

reference: MUSSULMANS AND GIAOURS THROW KERCHIEFS AT A SMILE AND HAVE NO RUTH FOR ANY WEEPING
hypothesis: Mussulmans and gays throw kerchiefs at a smile and have no ruth for any weeping.

reference: BUT THOU ART NOT SUCH A LOVER MY BELOVED
hypothesis: But thou art not such a lover, my beloved.

hypothesis: A narrow channel formed a passage through the ridge of rocks that protected it from the open sea, and which even in the roughest weather would ensure the calmness of its waters.

hypothesis: Slightly changing her course, she 1st struck her mainsail, and in order to facilitate the movements of her helmsman, soon carried nothing but her two topsails, brigantine and jib.

hypothesis: Captain Servadac hastened toward him.

hypothesis: Nothing more than you know yourself.

hypothesis: Are you certain that this is the Mediterranean?

hypothesis: Before starting, it was indispensable that the engine of the Dobryna should be repaired. To sail under canvas only would in contrary winds and rough seas be both tedious and difficult.

reference: IT WAS ON THE LAST DAY OF JANUARY THAT THE REPAIRS OF THE SCHOONER WERE COMPLETED
hypothesis: It was on the last day of January that the repairs of the schooner were completed.

reference: A SLIGHT DIMINUTION IN THE EXCESSIVELY HIGH TEMPERATURE WHICH HAD PREVAILED FOR THE LAST FEW WEEKS WAS THE ONLY APPARENT CHANGE IN THE GENERAL ORDER OF THINGS BUT WHETHER THIS WAS TO BE ATTRIBUTED TO ANY ALTERATION IN THE EARTH'S ORBIT WAS A QUESTION WHICH WOULD STILL REQUIRE SEVERAL DAYS TO DECIDE
hypothesis: A slight diminution in the excessively high temperature which had prevailed for the last few weeks was the only apparent change in the general order of things. But whether this was to be attributed to any alteration in the Earth's orbit was a question which would still require several days to decide.

reference: DOUBTS NOW AROSE AND SOME DISCUSSION FOLLOWED WHETHER OR NOT IT WAS DESIRABLE FOR BEN ZOOF TO ACCOMPANY HIS MASTER
hypothesis: Doubts now arose and some discussion followed whether or not it was desirable for Ben Zoof to accompany his master.

reference: LENGTH OF SERVICE FOURTEEN YEARS THREE MONTHS AND FIVE DAYS
hypothesis: Length of service 14 years, three months and five days.

reference: HE SEEMED BORN TO PLEASE WITHOUT BEING CONSCIOUS OF THE POWER HE POSSESSED
hypothesis: He seemed born to please without being conscious of the power he possessed.

reference: IT MUST BE OWNED AND NO ONE WAS MORE READY TO CONFESS IT THAN HIMSELF THAT HIS LITERARY ATTAINMENTS WERE BY NO MEANS OF A HIGH ORDER
hypothesis: It must be owned, and no one was more ready to confess it than himself.

reference: WE DON'T SPIN TOPS IS A FAVORITE SAYING AMONGST ARTILLERY OFFICERS INDICATING THAT THEY DO NOT SHIRK THEIR DUTY BY FRIVOLOUS PURSUITS BUT IT MUST BE CONFESSED THAT SERVADAC BEING NATURALLY IDLE WAS VERY MUCH GIVEN TO SPINNING TOPS
hypothesis: We don't spin tops is a favorite saying amongst artillery officers, indicating that they do not shirk their duty by frivolous pursuits. But it must be confessed that Servadac, being naturally idle, was very much given to spinning tops.

reference: ONCE IN ACTION HE WAS LEADING A DETACHMENT OF INFANTRY THROUGH AN INTRENCHMENT
hypothesis: Once in action, he was leading a detachment of infantry through an entrenchment.

hypothesis: Sometimes he would wander on foot upon the sandy shore, and sometimes he would enjoy a ride along the summit of the cliff, altogether being in no hurry at all to bring his task to an end.

reference: NO CATHEDRAL NOT EVEN BURGOS ITSELF COULD VIE WITH THE CHURCH AT MONTMARTRE
hypothesis: No cathedral, not even Burgos itself, could vie with the church at Montmartre.

reference: BEN ZOOF'S MOST AMBITIOUS DESIRE WAS TO INDUCE THE CAPTAIN TO GO WITH HIM AND END HIS DAYS IN HIS MUCH LOVED HOME AND SO INCESSANTLY WERE SERVADAC'S EARS BESIEGED WITH DESCRIPTIONS OF THE UNPARALLELED BEAUTIES AND ADVANTAGES OF THIS EIGHTEENTH ARRONDISSEMENT OF PARIS THAT HE COULD SCARCELY HEAR THE NAME OF MONTMARTRE WITHOUT A CONSCIOUS THRILL OF AVERSION
hypothesis: Ben Z's most ambitious desire was to induce the captain to go with him and end his days in his much loved home. And so incessantly were Servadac's ears besieged with descriptions of the unparalleled beauties and advantages of this 18th arrondissement of Paris that he could scarcely hear the name of Montmartre without a conscious thrill of aversion.

reference: WHEN A PRIVATE IN THE EIGHTH CAVALRY HE HAD BEEN ON THE POINT OF QUITTING THE ARMY AT TWENTY EIGHT YEARS OF AGE BUT UNEXPECTEDLY HE HAD BEEN APPOINTED ORDERLY TO CAPTAIN SERVADAC
hypothesis: When a private in the 8th Cavalry, he had been on the point of quitting the army at 28 years of age, but unexpectedly he had been appointed orderly to Captain Servadac.

reference: THE BOND OF UNION THUS EFFECTED COULD NEVER BE SEVERED AND ALTHOUGH BEN ZOOF'S ACHIEVEMENTS HAD FAIRLY EARNED HIM THE RIGHT OF RETIREMENT HE FIRMLY DECLINED ALL HONORS OR ANY PENSION THAT MIGHT PART HIM FROM HIS SUPERIOR OFFICER
hypothesis: The bond of union thus effected could never be severed, and although Ben Z's achievements had fairly earned him the right of retirement, he firmly declined all honors or any pension that might part him from his superior officer.

reference: UNLIKE HIS MASTER HE MADE NO PRETENSION TO ANY GIFT OF POETIC POWER BUT HIS INEXHAUSTIBLE MEMORY MADE HIM A LIVING ENCYCLOPAEDIA AND FOR HIS STOCK OF ANECDOTES AND TROOPER'S TALES HE WAS MATCHLESS
hypothesis: Unlike his master, he made no pretension to any gift of poetic power, but his inexhaustible memory made him a living encyclopedia, and for his stock of anecdotes and troopers tales he was matchless.

reference: TO CELEBRATE THE ARRIVAL OF HER SON SILVIA GAVE A SPLENDID SUPPER TO WHICH SHE HAD INVITED ALL HER RELATIVES AND IT WAS A GOOD OPPORTUNITY FOR ME TO MAKE THEIR ACQUAINTANCE
hypothesis: To celebrate the arrival of her son, Sylvia gave a splendid supper to which she had invited all her relatives and it was a good opportunity for me to make their acquaintance.

reference: WITHOUT SAYING IT POSITIVELY SHE MADE ME UNDERSTAND THAT BEING HERSELF AN ILLUSTRIOUS MEMBER OF THE REPUBLIC OF LETTERS SHE WAS WELL AWARE THAT SHE WAS SPEAKING TO AN INSECT
hypothesis: Without saying it positively, she made me understand that being herself an illustrious member of the Republic of Letters, she was well aware that she was speaking to an insect.

reference: IN ORDER TO PLEASE HER I SPOKE TO HER OF THE ABBE CONTI AND I HAD OCCASION TO QUOTE TWO LINES OF THAT PROFOUND WRITER
hypothesis: In order to please her, I spoke to her of the Abbey Conti and I had occasion to quote two lines of that profound writer.

reference: MADAM CORRECTED ME WITH A PATRONIZING AIR FOR MY PRONUNCIATION OF THE WORD SCEVRA WHICH MEANS DIVIDED SAYING THAT IT OUGHT TO BE PRONOUNCED SCEURA AND SHE ADDED THAT I OUGHT TO BE VERY GLAD TO HAVE LEARNED SO MUCH ON THE FIRST DAY OF MY ARRIVAL IN PARIS TELLING ME THAT IT WOULD BE AN IMPORTANT DAY IN MY LIFE
hypothesis: Madame corrected me with a patronizing air for my pronunciation of the word Sv, which means divided, saying that it ought to be pronounced surratt. to be very glad to have learned so much on the 1st day of my arrival in Paris, telling me that it would be an important day in my life.

reference: HER FACE WAS AN ENIGMA FOR IT INSPIRED EVERYONE WITH THE WARMEST SYMPATHY AND YET IF YOU EXAMINED IT ATTENTIVELY THERE WAS NOT ONE BEAUTIFUL FEATURE SHE COULD NOT BE CALLED HANDSOME BUT NO ONE COULD HAVE THOUGHT HER UGLY
hypothesis: Her face was an enigma, for it inspired everyone with the warmest sympathy. And yet if you examined it attentively, there was not one beautiful feature.

reference: SILVIA WAS THE ADORATION OF FRANCE AND HER TALENT WAS THE REAL SUPPORT OF ALL THE COMEDIES WHICH THE GREATEST AUTHORS WROTE FOR HER ESPECIALLY OF THE PLAYS OF MARIVAUX FOR WITHOUT HER HIS COMEDIES WOULD NEVER HAVE GONE TO POSTERITY
hypothesis: Sylvia was the adoration of France and her talent was the real support of all the comedies which the greatest authors wrote for her, especially of the plays of Marya, for without her his comedies would never have gone to posterity.

reference: SILVIA DID NOT THINK THAT HER GOOD CONDUCT WAS A MERIT FOR SHE KNEW THAT SHE WAS VIRTUOUS ONLY BECAUSE HER SELF LOVE COMPELLED HER TO BE SO AND SHE NEVER EXHIBITED ANY PRIDE OR ASSUMED ANY SUPERIORITY TOWARDS HER THEATRICAL SISTERS ALTHOUGH SATISFIED TO SHINE BY THEIR TALENT OR THEIR BEAUTY THEY CARED LITTLE ABOUT RENDERING THEMSELVES CONSPICUOUS BY THEIR VIRTUE
hypothesis: Sylvia did not think that her good conduct was a merit, for she knew that she was virtuous only because her self love compelled her to be so, and she never exhibited any pride or assumed any superiority towards her theatrical sisters.

reference: TWO YEARS BEFORE HER DEATH I SAW HER PERFORM THE CHARACTER OF MARIANNE IN THE COMEDY OF MARIVAUX AND IN SPITE OF HER AGE AND DECLINING HEALTH THE ILLUSION WAS COMPLETE
hypothesis: Two years before her death, I saw her perform the character of Marianne in the comedy of Mave. And in spite of her age and declining health, the illusion was complete.

reference: SHE WAS HONOURABLY BURIED IN THE CHURCH OF SAINT SAUVEUR WITHOUT THE SLIGHTEST OPPOSITION FROM THE VENERABLE PRIEST WHO FAR FROM SHARING THE ANTI CHRISTAIN INTOLERANCY OF THE CLERGY IN GENERAL SAID THAT HER PROFESSION AS AN ACTRESS HAD NOT HINDERED HER FROM BEING A GOOD CHRISTIAN AND THAT THE EARTH WAS THE COMMON MOTHER OF ALL HUMAN BEINGS AS JESUS CHRIST HAD BEEN THE SAVIOUR OF ALL MANKIND
hypothesis: She was honorably buried in the church of St. Sauveur without the slightest opposition from the venerable priest, who, far from sharing the anti Christian intolerance of the clergy in general.

hypothesis: You will forgive me, dear reader, if I have made you attend the funeral of Sylvia ten years before her death. Believe me, I have no intention of performing a miracle.

hypothesis: I never had any family.

hypothesis: I had a name, I believe, in my young days, but I have forgotten it since I have been in service.

hypothesis: Then turning towards me, he says that I look like a foreigner. and when I say that I am an Italian, he begins to speak to me of the court, the city, of the theatres, and at last he offers to accompany me everywhere.

hypothesis: I thank him and take my leave.

hypothesis: Simply by stopping her carriage two or three times before the shop to have her snuff box filled and by saying aloud to the young girl who handed back the box that her snuff was the very best in Paris.

hypothesis: Let a man run and everybody will run after him. The crowd will not stop unless the man is proved to be mad. But to prove it is indeed a difficult task because we have a crowd of men who, mad from their birth, are still considered wise.

hypothesis: It seems to me, I replied, that such approval, such ratification of the opinion expressed by the King, the princes of the blood, etcetera, is rather a proof of the affection felt for them by the nation. For the French carry that affection to such an extent that they believe them infallible.

reference: WHEN THE KING COMES TO PARIS EVERYBODY CALLS OUT VIVE LE ROI
hypothesis: When the king comes to Paris, everybody calls out, Vive le Roy.

hypothesis: She introduced me to all her guests and gave me some particulars respecting every one of them.

hypothesis: What sir? I said to him.

hypothesis: I am a very unpleasant pupil, always asking questions, curious, troublesome, insatiable, and even supposing that I could meet with the teacher I require.

hypothesis: I will make you translate them into French and you need not be afraid of my finding you insatiable.

hypothesis: He had a good appetite, could tell a good story without laughing, was celebrated for his witty repartees and his sociable manners. But he spent his life at home, seldom going out and seeing hardly anyone because he always had a pipe in his mouth and was surrounded by at least 20 cats with which he would amuse himself all day.

reference: HIS HOUSEKEEPER HAD THE MANAGEMENT OF EVERYTHING SHE NEVER ALLOWED HIM TO BE IN NEED OF ANYTHING AND SHE GAVE NO ACCOUNT OF HIS MONEY WHICH SHE KEPT ALTOGETHER BECAUSE HE NEVER ASKED HER TO RENDER ANY ACCOUNTS
hypothesis: His housekeeper had the management of everything.

reference: HE COULD WAIT NO LONGER
hypothesis: He could wait no longer.

reference: FOR A FULL HOUR HE HAD PACED UP AND DOWN WAITING BUT HE COULD WAIT NO LONGER
hypothesis: For a full hour he had paced up and down waiting, but he could wait no longer.

reference: HE SET OFF ABRUPTLY FOR THE BULL WALKING RAPIDLY LEST HIS FATHER'S SHRILL WHISTLE MIGHT CALL HIM BACK AND IN A FEW MOMENTS HE HAD ROUNDED THE CURVE AT THE POLICE BARRACK AND WAS SAFE
hypothesis: He set off abruptly for the bull, walking rapidly lest his father's shrill whistle might call him back, and in a few moments he had rounded the curve at the police barrack and was safe.

reference: THE UNIVERSITY
hypothesis: The University

reference: PRIDE AFTER SATISFACTION UPLIFTED HIM LIKE LONG SLOW WAVES
hypothesis: Pride after satisfaction uplifted him like long slow waves.

reference: WHOSE FEET ARE AS THE FEET OF HARTS AND UNDERNEATH THE EVERLASTING ARMS

hypothesis: The pride of that dim image brought back to his mind the dignity of the office he had refused.

reference: SOON THE WHOLE BRIDGE WAS TREMBLING AND RESOUNDING
hypothesis: Soon the whole bridge was trembling and resounding.

hypothesis: The uncouth faces passed him two by two, stained yellow or red or livid by the sea, and as he strove to look at them with ease and indifference, a faint stain of personal shame and commiseration rose to his own face.

reference: ANGRY WITH HIMSELF HE TRIED TO HIDE HIS FACE FROM THEIR EYES BY GAZING DOWN SIDEWAYS INTO THE SHALLOW SWIRLING WATER UNDER THE BRIDGE BUT HE STILL SAW A REFLECTION THEREIN OF THEIR TOP HEAVY SILK HATS AND HUMBLE TAPE LIKE COLLARS AND LOOSELY HANGING CLERICAL CLOTHES BROTHER HICKEY
hypothesis: Angry with himself, he tried to hide his face from their eyes by gazing down sideways into the shallow swirling water under the bridge, but he still saw a reflection therein of their top heavy silk hats and humble tape like collars and loosely hanging clerical clothes.

reference: BROTHER MAC ARDLE BROTHER KEOGH
hypothesis: Brother Mcardle, Brother Ko.

reference: THEIR PIETY WOULD BE LIKE THEIR NAMES LIKE THEIR FACES LIKE THEIR CLOTHES AND IT WAS IDLE FOR HIM TO TELL HIMSELF THAT THEIR HUMBLE AND CONTRITE HEARTS IT MIGHT BE PAID A FAR RICHER TRIBUTE OF DEVOTION THAN HIS HAD EVER BEEN A GIFT TENFOLD MORE ACCEPTABLE THAN HIS ELABORATE ADORATION
hypothesis: Their piety would be like their names, like their faces, like their clothes. And it was idle for him to tell himself that their humble and contrite hearts, it might be, paid a far richer tribute of devotion than his had ever been, a gift tenfold more acceptable than his elaborate adoration.

reference: IT WAS IDLE FOR HIM TO MOVE HIMSELF TO BE GENEROUS TOWARDS THEM TO TELL HIMSELF THAT IF HE EVER CAME TO THEIR GATES STRIPPED OF HIS PRIDE BEATEN AND IN BEGGAR'S WEEDS THAT THEY WOULD BE GENEROUS TOWARDS HIM LOVING HIM AS THEMSELVES
hypothesis: It was idle for him to move himself, to be generous towards them, to tell himself that if he ever came to their gates, stripped of his pride, beaten and in beggars weeds, that they would be generous towards him, loving him as themselves.

reference: IDLE AND EMBITTERING FINALLY TO ARGUE AGAINST HIS OWN DISPASSIONATE CERTITUDE THAT THE COMMANDMENT OF LOVE BADE US NOT TO LOVE OUR NEIGHBOUR AS OURSELVES WITH THE SAME AMOUNT AND INTENSITY OF LOVE BUT TO LOVE HIM AS OURSELVES WITH THE SAME KIND OF LOVE
hypothesis: Idle and embitter. Finally, to argue against his own dispassionate certitude that the commandment of love bade us not to love our neighbor as ourselves with the same amount and intensity of love, but to love him as ourselves with the same kind of love.

reference: THE PHRASE AND THE DAY AND THE SCENE HARMONIZED IN A CHORD
hypothesis: The phrase and the day and the scene harmonized in accord.

hypothesis: Words

hypothesis: Their diving stone poised on its rude supports and rocking under their plunges, and the rough hewn stones of the sloping breakwater over which they scrambled in their horseplay gleamed with cold, wet lustre.

hypothesis: Then all our good times are over.

hypothesis: Every chance she could steal after practice hours were over and after the clamorous demands of the boys upon her time were fully satisfied, was seized to fly on the wings of the wind to the flowers.

hypothesis: Then, dear, said Mrs. Whitney, you must be kinder to her than ever. Think what it would be for one of you to be away from home, even among friends.

hypothesis: Somehow, of all the days when the home feeling was the strongest, this day it seemed as if she could bear it no longer.

hypothesis: At this the bundle opened suddenly and

hypothesis: Isn't he splendid? cried Jasper in intense pride, swelling up. Father knew how to do it.

hypothesis: I know, gasped Polly, controlling her sobs. I won't only.

hypothesis: Asked Phronsie in intense interest, slipping down out of Polly's arms and crowding up close to Jasper's side.

hypothesis: Yes, all alone by himself, asserted Jasper vehemently, and winking furiously to the others to stop their laughing. He did. Now truly, Phronsie.

hypothesis: Don't mind it, Polly, whispered Jasper. Twasn't her fault.

hypothesis: Dear me. ejaculated the old gentleman in the utmost amazement. And such a time as I've had to get her here too.

reference: HOW DID HER MOTHER EVER LET HER GO
hypothesis: How did her mother ever let her go?

hypothesis: She asked impulsively. I didn't believe you could persuade her father.

hypothesis: I didn't have any fears if I worked it rightly, said the old gentleman complacently.

hypothesis: He cried in high dudgeon, just as if he owned the whole of the peppers and could dispose of them all to suit his fancy.
